subject: Support for init loop device.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md:326 — Class B: loop= root patch, disposition 'delete — replaced by initramfs (§5)'

impact.effect_if_absent: In 5.15 fork: the loop= kernel parameter would not be recognized, /dev/loop8 would not be created, and loop-mounted root images would not boot. In 6.18 forward port: functionality is provided by initramfs instead; effect is silent—users would not see an error if they passed loop= to a kernel without this patch.

superseded_by: initramfs /init boot flow with loop= parameter parsing (PLAN §5)

notes: Class B commit in patch-provenance.md. Deliberately not forward-ported to 6.18; functionality is being replaced by an initramfs-based boot flow per PLAN §5 and patch-provenance.md §4 analysis. Exports loop_max_part() from drivers/block/loop.c and adds loop_setup() and loop= parameter parsing to init/do_mounts.c. No userspace coupling confirmed via Main_MiSTer audit (no references to /root2, /dev/loop8, or LOOP_SET_FD ioctl). Disposition and grounding align with patch-provenance.md baseline. sonnet-verified: correct — diff re-confirmed against the fork commit exactly (loop_max_part() export in drivers/block/loop.c, loop= __setup + m_open/loop_setup + mount_root() rewrite in init/do_mounts.c); patch-provenance.md §4's 11-point line-by-line coverage table substantiates the '100% of the loop= patch's own logic' claim; grep for root2/loop8/LOOP_SET_FD in Main_MiSTer returns zero hits, confirming no userspace coupling.
